_doc.t510_canonical_core: T510: Gemini CLI reads lifecycle hooks from the `hooks` object in `.gemini/settings.json` (Claude-style: per-event array of { matcher, hooks:[{type:command,command,...}] }; tool-event matchers are regex, lifecycle matchers exact, `*` or `` matches all; timeout in ms). Each native event routes to a thin canonical entrypoint `python .gemini/hooks/g-hk-on-<event>.py` which calls `g_hk_core.dispatch("<event>")`. The shared core `.gemini/hooks/g_hk_core.py` is byte-identical across every platform overlay and runs the per-event CONCERN_CHAIN (concern hooks self-gate on payload). Native->canonical map (g_hk_core.PLATFORM_EVENT_MAP['gemini']): SessionStart->session-start, SessionEnd->session-end, BeforeTool->tool-start, AfterTool->tool-end, AfterAgent->stop. Schema verified against https://geminicli.com/docs/hooks/ .
_doc.overlay_note: T510: gemini's REAL native dot-dir is `.gemini/` (this file). The pre-existing `platforms/gemini/.agent/` overlay is a Claude-format clone the gemini product does NOT read; reconciling/removing it is tracked separately (see T510 Status History) and is OUT OF SCOPE for the hook fan-out.
